creators.name: Nick Saraev
creators.blurb: Practical AI automation and agency builds from someone shipping real systems.

videos.title: I Built a $1M/y SaaS with Claude Code, Here's How
videos.summary: Nick Saraev describes building Clairvo, a $1M/year SaaS that uses Claude Code-generated techniques like predictive pacing and local number rotation to improve call pickup rates. He argues that using Claude to enumerate opportunities and practical implementation of AI agents is viable for building real-world revenue-generating businesses, contrasting his approach with many who talk automation without results.

videos.title: How to Build Mobile Apps with Claude Code: Full Course (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev's full-length course teaches mobile app development using Claude Code and Expo/React Native. The course covers setup, app design frameworks, building three complete apps (habit tracker, calorie tracker with AI, Pomodoro timer), database integration with Supabase, user authentication, and app store submission.

videos.title: How to Use Claude Code for FREE (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev demonstrates how to use Claude Code for free or at minimal cost by routing through NVIDIA-NIM, OpenRouter, Ollama for local models, and alternatives like DeepSeek V4 Flash. The video includes setup instructions for each method and references a full 4-hour Claude Code course.

videos.title: Claude Opus-4.7 Just Dropped, And...
videos.summary: Claude Opus-4.7 is the latest version of Anthropic's Claude model. Nick Saraev discusses the release and its applications in a YouTube video covering practical automation workflows and business use cases.

creators.name: AI Engineer
creators.blurb: Talks and sessions from the AI Engineer conference and community.

videos.title: GPU Cloud Deployment Without Leaving Your IDE — Audry Hsu, RunPod
videos.summary: Flash is a framework for deploying Python functions to GPU cloud with a single decorator. It replaces multi-step workflows with hot reload and eliminates container rebuild cycles. RunPod offers both pod and serverless deployment modes with pay-per-second H100 pricing.

videos.title: RAG is dead, right?? — Kuba Rogut, Turbopuffer
videos.summary: Kuba Rogut argues that RAG is not dead despite social media discourse declaring it so, pointing to rising Google search volume and real improvements from semantic search in Cursor. The core issue was a narrow definition of retrieval, which should include vector search, full text search, glob, regex, and filters used iteratively by agents. The key tradeoff is whether to cache embeddings based on query volume, as exemplified by Cursor's upfront indexing versus Claude Code's per-session grep approach.

videos.title: From Transcription to Live Music: Gemini's Audio Stack — Thor Schaeff, Google DeepMind
videos.summary: Google DeepMind's Gemini audio stack provides one-API access to audio understanding with speaker labels, emotion detection, and language identification. It integrates speech generation via director's notes and Gemini 3.1 Flash Live, a multimodal sound-to-sound real-time model with integrated reasoning. The stack includes Lyria 3 for full song generation with lyrics, callable via tool use from Gemini Live.

videos.title: 2026 AI Engineer Vibe Reel
videos.summary: AI Engineer is promoting the 2026 World's Fair in San Francisco scheduled for June 29 to July 2. The post directs viewers to ai.engineer/wf for tickets and schedule information.

creators.name: Cole Medin
creators.blurb: Practical AI agent and coding-assistant builds, end to end.

videos.title: Live AI Q&A + Crushing it in Chess at the Same Time - Come Hang Out!
videos.summary: Cole Medin is hosting a live Q&A session on AI coding, agents, and Claude Code on Saturday mornings. The stream will cover topics like AI agents and Archon while the host plays chess simultaneously.

videos.title: Claude Plans, Gemini Designs: The Workflow to Build BEAUTIFUL Frontends
videos.summary: Cole Medin presents a multi-model workflow that routes frontend development tasks to specialized models: Claude Opus for planning and reasoning, Gemini 3.5 Flash for UI design and implementation due to superior visual output and lower cost. The approach demonstrates practical composition of recent flagship models released in the same week.

videos.title: Claude Plans, Gemini Designs: One Workflow for Beautiful Frontends (LIVE)
videos.summary: Cole Medin demonstrates a workflow that chains Claude Opus and Gemini 3.5 Flash for frontend development, with Opus handling planning and copy while Gemini handles UI design. The Archon orchestration framework enables this multi-provider coordination for rapid, visually polished application builds.

videos.title: Harness Engineering: What Separates Top Agentic Engineers Right Now
videos.summary: Harness engineering is the practice of building wrapper systems around LLMs to create reliable coding agents. It operates at two levels: the AI layer governing single sessions through rules and hooks, and multi-session orchestration that chains focused agents into larger workflows. The harness comprises roughly 98% of production systems like Claude Code.

creators.name: AI Jason
creators.blurb: AI app and agent builds, experiments, and walkthroughs.

videos.title: How to build proactive agents & self-improving company (Fully explained)
videos.summary: Video explains how to build proactive AI agents and self-improving company systems. Covers autonomous loops for ads optimization and SEO, featuring tools like Loopany that use memory and tool integration to enable continuous agent iteration.

videos.title: Ralph-loop 2.0? The real autonomous coder is coming...
videos.summary: AI Jason discusses emerging autonomous coding features including the /goal feature and /Mission exploration for long-horizon task execution. The video covers goal-setting practices and workflow setup for advanced code generation tools.

videos.title: New AI coding paradiagm - OpenAI Symphony
videos.summary: AI Jason discusses OpenAI Symphony, a new coding paradigm that integrates AI into software development workflows. The video covers how Symphony works, harness setup with auto-testing, and configuration through WORKFLOW.md files.

videos.title: Okay, this unleashed my agent
videos.summary: AI Jason reviews multiple autonomous agent frameworks and mechanisms for building AI agents, comparing approaches like autoresearch, self-evolution, and memory architectures. The video covers Claude Code, Hermes, and OpenClaw as implementations.

creators.name: Caleb Writes Code
creators.blurb: Hands-on coding sessions and software build-alongs.

videos.title: MiniMax M3 explained in 8min..
videos.summary: MiniMax released M3, a language model featuring MiniMax Sparse Attention (MSA) for efficient inference. The model incorporates tiled KV cache optimizations and I/O improvements that reduce memory bandwidth requirements during prefill and decode stages by enabling single continuous reads.

videos.title: Pi Agent explained in 6min..
videos.summary: Pi is described as a unique agent framework that enables various use cases like OpenClaw. The video compares Pi against other frontier agents including Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, and Antigravity, discussing how these tools handle increasingly complex harness layer features.

videos.title: Huawei makes 1.4nm chip??
videos.summary: Caleb Writes Code discusses Huawei's announcement of Tau Scaling Law as a competitive response in the hardware and semiconductor race between China and the USA. The video covers advances in EUV and DUV lithography, packaging, and logic folding as key enablers of progress in chip manufacturing.

videos.title: Agent Harness explained in 8min..
videos.summary: Caleb Writes Code explains agent harness engineering as an evolution in agentic AI systems, covering the progression from prompt engineering and context engineering through loops and architecture. The video positions harness engineering as a growing clarification on how agentic systems are designed and controlled.

creators.name: AWS Developers
creators.blurb: Official AWS tutorials, service deep dives, and developer demos.

videos.title: How to Beat AI in Your Dev Job Search
videos.summary: AWS Developers video addresses how developers can stand out in job searches as AI screens resumes, emphasizing networking, personal branding, and open-source contributions. Argues junior developers remain valuable despite automation, using job evolution analogies and recommending hands-on learning paths.

videos.title: Host any coding agent on AgentCore
videos.summary: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore is a service that enables hosting and running multiple coding agents including Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, and Kiro. Users can deploy any coding agent on the platform via the Bedrock service.

videos.title: 5 Tips for Deploying AI Agents to Production
videos.summary: AWS tutorial covers five production deployment patterns for AI agents built with TypeScript: streaming tool call events for UI responsiveness, layered authentication combining OAuth and IAM, type-safe database access with tenant isolation, guardrails with cost budgets and model routing, and observability via metrics and OpenTelemetry tracing.

videos.title: Infrastructure as Code for Beginners (AWS CDK)
videos.summary: A tutorial on Infrastructure as Code using AWS CDK with Python to build a serverless REST API. The video contrasts manual AWS console configuration with declarative infrastructure code, covering DynamoDB, Lambda, and API Gateway deployment.

creators.name: Visual AI
creators.blurb: Visual walkthroughs and demos across modern AI tools.

creators.name: Alberta Tech
creators.blurb: Developer tutorials and practical builds in AI and software.

videos.title: How Meta AI accidentally gave away Obama's instagram
videos.summary: A video by Alberta Tech discusses an incident where Meta AI reportedly exposed or provided unauthorized access to Barack Obama's Instagram account. The framing suggests an unintended disclosure or security failure by Meta's AI system.

videos.title: Do Google engineers actually vibe code?
videos.summary: Alberta Tech compares code generation capabilities across tools and explores whether Google engineers are effectively adopting or benefiting from AI-assisted coding in 2024-2026. The video discusses code generation practices at Google and contrasts OpenAI and Claude's code generation approaches.

videos.title: CEO finds out tokens cost money
videos.summary: A YouTube video from Alberta Tech presenting a comedic take on the realization that LLM token consumption carries significant operational costs. The video highlights the financial implications of token-based pricing models for businesses deploying AI systems.

videos.title: He's a marketing "engineer"!? 🤨
videos.summary: Alberta Tech questions the legitimacy or appropriateness of someone being called a marketing engineer. The dismissive tone suggests skepticism about role definitions or title inflation in tech.

creators.name: EO
creators.blurb: Conversations and stories with founders and builders in tech.

videos.title: The Fastest Way to Know if Your Product Market Fit Is Real | Serval CEO, Jake Stauch
videos.summary: Jake Stauch, CEO of Serval, discusses how to distinguish genuine product-market fit from delusion. He argues that rabid fans and formal customer interviews are unreliable signals, and instead advocates for embedded customer relationships and assessing whether customers actively pull the product rather than requiring sales push.

videos.title: He Raised $26M Betting on Probabilities, Not Guesses | Koah Labs, Nic Baird
videos.summary: Nic Baird, founder of Koah Labs, argues that ad-based monetization is more viable than subscription models for AI tools and that the real bottleneck for AI agents is human trust rather than technology. He shares insights on decision-making under uncertainty drawn from his background in competitive sailing.

videos.title: I Hit $1M ARR in 117 Days. Bootstrapped to $10M. Here's My Playbook | Chatbase, Yasser Elsaid
videos.summary: Yasser Elsaid, founder of Chatbase, shares his bootstrapping playbook that achieved $1M ARR in 117 days and grew to $10M ARR without external funding. The episode covers strategies including PLG, SEO and AEO, warm outbound, churn reduction, and pricing experimentation.

videos.title: A CS Professor on Why Slow Learning Wins in the AI Era | CU Boulder, Tom Yeh
videos.summary: Tom Yeh argues for teaching AI through hand-written mathematical foundations at human speed rather than focusing on latest tools. He contends that strong fundamentals survive hype cycles and that people, not AI, drive meaningful change in AI development.

creators.name: Cyril Imhof
creators.blurb: AI automation and workflow builds for real projects.

videos.title: AI Supercharges Sales & Unlocks Scientific Breakthroughs! #shorts
videos.summary: A short-form video argues that AI is revolutionizing sales preparation and deal closing while accelerating scientific discovery. The content emphasizes AI's transformative potential across commercial and research domains.

videos.title: AI Agents: Unlocking Long-Term Autonomy & Realizing Value Now! #shorts
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof argues that new AI models enable long-horizon autonomy in agents, allowing complex tasks to be delegated and unlocking significant value. The post presents an optimistic view of AI agents taking on greater responsibility.

videos.title: AI: Scientific Breakthroughs, Digital Identity, and Agent Governance! #shorts
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof explores AI's impact across scientific discovery, sales applications, and the emergence of autonomous digital identities. The piece emphasizes the importance of agent governance and enterprise authorization strategies for managing AI systems at scale.

videos.title: Fable 5.0: Mythos Power for Builders - But Is It Ready? #shorts
videos.summary: A video comparing Fable 5.0 with Opus 4.8, questioning whether Fable 5.0 is ready for builders. The creator explores its limited capabilities and potential use in detecting malicious activities.

creators.name: PY
creators.blurb: Software engineering, tooling, and developer-workflow deep dives.
